topology.h(3) Library Functions Manual topology.h(3)

NAME

topology.h - Ring topology management.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t consensus_topology_create (const uint8_t participant_ids[64][16], int num_participants, const uint8_t my_id[16], consensus_topology_t **out_topology)
Create new ring topology from participant list. void consensus_topology_destroy (consensus_topology_t *topology)
Destroy topology. int consensus_topology_get_position (const consensus_topology_t *topology)
Get my position in ring (0 = first). bool consensus_topology_am_leader (const consensus_topology_t *topology)
Check if I am the ring leader (last position). asciichat_error_t consensus_topology_get_next (const consensus_topology_t *topology, uint8_t out_id[16])
Get next participant ID in ring. asciichat_error_t consensus_topology_get_prev (const consensus_topology_t *topology, uint8_t out_id[16])
Get previous participant ID in ring. asciichat_error_t consensus_topology_get_leader (const consensus_topology_t *topology, uint8_t out_id[16])
Get ring leader ID (last participant). asciichat_error_t consensus_topology_get_all (const consensus_topology_t *topology, uint8_t out_ids[64][16], int *out_count)
Get all participant IDs in ring order.

Detailed Description

Ring topology management.

Definition in file topology.h.

Function Documentation

bool consensus_topology_am_leader (const consensus_topology_t * topology)

Check if I am the ring leader (last position).

Parameters

topology Topology handle

Returns

true if I am the leader, false otherwise

Definition at line 76 of file topology.c.

76                                                                         {
77   if (!topology)
78     return false;
79   return topology->my_position == (topology->num_participants - 1);
80 }




References consensus_topology::my_position, and
    consensus_topology::num_participants.


Referenced by consensus_coordinator_process(),
    consensus_state_collection_complete(), and
    consensus_state_is_leader().






asciichat_error_t consensus_topology_create (const
  uint8_t participant_ids[64][16], int num_participants, const
  uint8_t my_id[16], consensus_topology_t ** out_topology)


Create new ring topology from participant list. Sorts participants
    lexicographically and computes ring positions. Returns error if my_id not in
    participant list.




Parameters


participant_ids Array of 16-byte UUIDs (up to 64
  participants)


num_participants Number of participants (1-64)


my_id My 16-byte UUID (must be in participant_ids)


out_topology Output topology handle (caller must destroy)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 38 of file topology.c.


39                                                                                                           {
40   if (!participant_ids || !my_id || !out_topology || num_participants <= 0 || num_participants > 64) {
41     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid topology creation parameters");
42   }
43 
44   consensus_topology_t *topology = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(*topology), consensus_topology_t *);
45   memset(topology, 0, sizeof(*topology));
46 
47   // Copy and sort participant IDs lexicographically
48   memcpy(topology->participant_ids, participant_ids, num_participants * 16);
49   topology->num_participants = num_participants;
50 
51   qsort(topology->participant_ids, num_participants, 16, qsort_uuid_compare);
52 
53   // Find my position
54   topology->my_position = find_uuid_position(topology->participant_ids, num_participants, my_id);
55   if (topology->my_position < 0) {
56     SAFE_FREE(topology);
57     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "My UUID not found in participant list");
58   }
59 
60   *out_topology = topology;
61   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
62 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    consensus_topology::my_position,
    consensus_topology::num_participants,
    consensus_topology::participant_ids, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by session_consensus_create(), and
    session_consensus_set_topology().






void
  consensus_topology_destroy (consensus_topology_t * topology)


Destroy topology.


Parameters


topology Topology handle (safe to pass NULL)

65   if (topology) {
66     SAFE_FREE(topology);
67   }
68 }

Referenced by session_consensus_create(),
    session_consensus_destroy(), and
    session_consensus_set_topology().






asciichat_error_t consensus_topology_get_all (const
  consensus_topology_t * topology, uint8_t out_ids[64][16], int *
  out_count)


Get all participant IDs in ring order.


Parameters


topology Topology handle


out_ids Array to fill with 16-byte UUIDs (must hold 64*16 bytes)


out_count Output number of participants


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 111 of file topology.c.


112                                                              {
113   if (!topology || !out_ids || !out_count) {
114     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
115   }
116 
117   memcpy(out_ids, topology->participant_ids, topology->num_participants * 16);
118   *out_count = topology->num_participants;
119   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
120 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    consensus_topology::num_participants,
    consensus_topology::participant_ids, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t consensus_topology_get_leader (const
  consensus_topology_t * topology, uint8_t out_id[16])


Get ring leader ID (last participant).


Parameters


topology Topology handle


out_id Output 16-byte UUID of ring leader


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 102 of file topology.c.


102                                                                                                           {
103   if (!topology || !out_id) {
104     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
105   }
106 
107   memcpy(out_id, topology->participant_ids[topology->num_participants - 1], 16);
108   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
109 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    consensus_topology::num_participants,
    consensus_topology::participant_ids, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t consensus_topology_get_next (const
  consensus_topology_t * topology, uint8_t out_id[16])


Get next participant ID in ring. Wraps around from last position
    to first.




Parameters


topology Topology handle


out_id Output 16-byte UUID of next participant


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 82 of file topology.c.


82                                                                                                         {
83   if (!topology || !out_id) {
84     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
85   }
86 
87   int next_pos = (topology->my_position + 1) % topology->num_participants;
88   memcpy(out_id, topology->participant_ids[next_pos], 16);
89   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
90 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    consensus_topology::my_position,
    consensus_topology::num_participants,
    consensus_topology::participant_ids, and SET_ERRNO.






int
  consensus_topology_get_position (const consensus_topology_t *
  topology)


Get my position in ring (0 = first).


Parameters


topology Topology handle


Returns


My position in ring order, or -1 if invalid


Definition at line 70 of file topology.c.


70                                                                           {
71   if (!topology)
72     return -1;
73   return topology->my_position;
74 }






References consensus_topology::my_position.






asciichat_error_t consensus_topology_get_prev (const
  consensus_topology_t * topology, uint8_t out_id[16])


Get previous participant ID in ring. Wraps around from first
    position to last.




Parameters


topology Topology handle


out_id Output 16-byte UUID of previous participant


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 92 of file topology.c.


92                                                                                                         {
93   if (!topology || !out_id) {
94     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
95   }
96 
97   int prev_pos = (topology->my_position - 1 + topology->num_participants) % topology->num_participants;
98   memcpy(out_id, topology->participant_ids[prev_pos], 16);
99   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
100 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    consensus_topology::my_position,
    consensus_topology::num_participants,
    consensus_topology::participant_ids, and SET_ERRNO.
